channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Offers $10 Million Reward for Information on Kataeb Sayyid al-Shuhada Leader
item.description: The US State Department is offering up to $10 million for information leading to the capture or location of Hashim Finyan Rahim al-Saraji, the leader of the Iranian-backed Iraqi militia group Kataeb S...

item.title: Professional Dog Walkers Share Their Top Sandals for Comfort During Long Walks
item.description: Professional dog walkers spend long hours on their feet, often in warm weather, making comfortable footwear essential. In this article, expert dog walkers Rachel Millman and Kimberly Reckles share the...

item.title: Negros Occidental Encounter: US Citizen Among Those Killed, Military and Rebel Groups Disagree on Details
item.description: A reported American citizen of Filipino descent was confirmed among the casualties in recent clashes between government forces and alleged New People's Army (NPA) members in Toboso, Negros Occidental....

item.title: Njugush Opens Up About Breakup With Wife and Family Matters
item.description: Comedian Njugush, whose real name is Timothy Kimani, has confirmed that he and his wife Celestine Ndinda, popularly known as Wakavinye, have officially parted ways. The couple, considered one of Kenya...

item.title: Millicent Omanga Shares Heartfelt Tribute to Daughter on 16th Birthday
item.description: Former nominated senator Millicent Omanga recently celebrated a special milestone—her daughter Maya Kerubo’s 16th birthday. Omanga, who shares Maya with her ex-husband Francis Nyamiobo, took to social...

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith to attend Queensland Anzac Day service while facing war crime charges
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, a former SAS corporal and Victoria Cross recipient, is set to attend an Anzac Day service in Queensland, marking his first appearance at the commemoration since being charged with m...

item.title: Amit Shah predicts BJP majority after high voter turnout in Bengal's first phase
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah expressed confidence in the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) achieving a clear majority in West Bengal following the first phase of Assembly elections, which saw a record vot...

item.title: NYT Report: US Weapons Stockpile Drained Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: According to a detailed report by the New York Times, the United States has utilised a massive portion of its weapons stockpile in the ongoing war against Iran. This includes over 1,200 Patriot interc...

item.title: Composer Brian Tyler reflects on near-death experience that shaped ‘Super Mario Galaxy Movie’ score
item.description: Film composer Brian Tyler revealed that the music for “The Super Mario Galaxy Movie” was deeply influenced by a life-threatening medical emergency he experienced in August 2025. While working in his s...

item.title: Australia Faces Uncertainty Over US Ambassador Appointment Amid Trump's Term
item.description: Fifteen months into Donald Trump’s presidency, Australia’s prolonged wait for a permanent US ambassador has entered a new phase. The current acting ambassador, Erika Olson, is set to depart her post i...

item.title: Unclaimed R8.5 million Lotto prize from Westville ticket
item.description: A Lotto Plus 2 ticket purchased in Westville on 20 September 2025 remains unclaimed, with the prize amounting to a life-changing R8.5 million. ITHUBA, the operator of the South African National Lotter...

item.title: Philadelphia Schools Introduce ‘Rallee’ Brain Break App Amid Questions About Effectiveness and Screen Time
item.description: The School District of Philadelphia has introduced a new app called Rallee to support “brain breaks” for students, short pauses during learning that involve physical or cognitive activities. The app e...

item.title: California law allows certain elderly inmates, including convicted child sex offenders, to seek early parole
item.description: A California law signed by Governor Gavin Newsom, which amended the Elderly Release Program, has led to controversy over the early parole eligibility of convicted child sex offenders. Israel Ceja, an ...

item.title: Pentagon Memo Considers Revisiting NATO Allies' Support and UK's Falkland Islands Claim Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: An internal Pentagon email has reportedly explored options to address NATO allies perceived as unsupportive during the US-led conflict with Iran. Among the measures considered are suspending Spain fro...

item.title: Thai Food Festival in Madurai Introduces New Culinary Flavours to Locals
item.description: A Thai food festival at Phil’s Bistro in KK Nagar, Madurai, is offering residents a refreshing shift from the city’s traditionally spicy and rich cuisine. Known for local favourites like kari dosa and...

item.title: Impact of School Closures on Students’ Education and Well-being
item.description: The article discusses the growing concern over unrecognised schools in India and their impact on students. When such schools suddenly close due to issues with affiliation, children face not just acade...

item.title: Dino Guglielmelli: Rise, Fall and Life After Prison
item.description: Dino Guglielmelli, once a billionaire entrepreneur and CEO of Creation's Garden, saw his financial empire collapse after a series of personal and legal challenges. His company, which generated around ...

item.title: Pakistan and Iran discuss ceasefire and Islamabad’s mediation efforts in US-Iran tensions
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar held a telephonic conversation with Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi to discuss the evolving regional situation, particularly the US-Iran cea...

item.title: Harry Styles and Zoe Kravitz announce engagement after eight months of dating
item.description: Harry Styles, the former One Direction singer, and actress Zoe Kravitz are reportedly engaged following an eight-month romance. The 32-year-old musician and the 37-year-old Batman star are said to be ...

item.title: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner advocates cooperation with China on climate change
item.description: Graham Platner, a Democratic candidate for the U.S. Senate from Maine, has proposed a more cooperative approach with China, emphasizing collaboration on global challenges such as climate change. Durin...
